name: Open Game RuleBook
description: Technology-agnostic game specification. The game IS this document. Any engine, renderer, or human GM can interpret it.
format: JSON (software-readable), with optional Markdown rendering for human consumption
design_principles: Everything is a skill — every action from breathing to casting a meteor is a skill defined here
Memories define what an entity CAN do — the list of skills available to it
The RuleBook is the single source of truth — engines are interchangeable interpreters
A session can start on a computer and continue on a tabletop — state is portable
No action is hardcoded in the engine — the engine reads and executes skills from this book

structure.memories/: Entity memory templates — lists of available skills per character
structure.controls/: Input bindings — which physical action triggers which skill
structure.scenes/: Scene state — positions, environment, and live state of every instance. Engine reads on load, writes back on change.

godot_data_mapping.note: Existing .tres files in data/ are the Godot-specific implementation of this RuleBook. This JSON is the technology-agnostic source; .tres files are derived from it.
